input.instruction: Assess whether the evidence package has the gap this pattern describes. If the package is complete for this COU, the correct disposition is to accept — do NOT over-act on an alarming-looking but adequately-addressed signal. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge.

fired_pattern.definition: Prediction UQ present but not validated.

case_context.cou: the wide band informs a flood-margin decision
case_context.decision_consequence: flood margin from the reported band

answer_key.gold_mechanism: The UQ-validation gap is absent: wide but calibrated intervals (coverage 0.91 vs nominal 0.90 in the COU regime, width from propagated genuine variability) are complete, honest evidence — there is no defeater to act on.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

parameters.justification: intervals wide but calibrated (0.91 vs 0.90), width is genuine propagated variability

forbidden_claims: restricts or rejects on the basis of wide-but-calibrated intervals (over-action)
asserts the surrogate is credible/correct (a verdict the package does not adjudicate)

hardness.obvious_signal: the prediction intervals are very wide — up to +/-40% of the mean
hardness.conflicting_signal: the wide intervals are validated: empirical coverage in the COU regime is 0.91 vs nominal 0.90, and the width reflects genuine hydrological variability that is propagated honestly

label_provenance.external_grounding: ASME V&V 40: calibrated uncertainty, however wide, is the desired UQ outcome; over-acting on calibrated width is the over-action error a control must catch.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
